repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
Teaches Cython about Python's exception class hierarchy and allows it to infer a concrete exception (base) type as spanning type for two given exceptions.

Moves the long list of known builtin type names from `Code.py` (where it always felt awkward) to `PyrexTypes.py`. `Builtin.py` might seem better but introduces import order issues since the list is used in other modules that `Builtin.py` depends on. It now lives right before `BuiltinObjectType` in `PyrexTypes.py`, which seems sufficiently appropriate.

I also moved `uncachable_builtins` with it to help keeping that set in sight of the known builtins.
I wonder if `renamed_py2_builtins_map` should be moved as well. It's not going to change any more.

commit.message: Let Cython know about Python's exception type hierarchy.
Infer the concrete exception base type as spanning type for two given exceptions.
Clean up, fix and extend tests.

title: Infer exception base types
